newsletter.markdown:
## News

**[FDA advisory panel weighs peptide compounding regulation](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/fda-advisory-panel-peptide-safety-compounding-pharmacies/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- FDA advisers meeting to decide whether permitting compounding pharmacies to supply largely unproven peptides is safer than the current gray-market supply.
- Central tension: patient access via compounding vs. absence of rigorous safety/efficacy data for these peptide products.
- Outcome will shape enforcement discretion for a rapidly growing peptide marketplace.

**[Lilly’s retatrutide (“triple‑G”) cardiovascular outcomes leave key question unanswered](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/new-data-on-eli-lilly-triple-g-drug-retatrutide-leave-open-questions-on-heart-benefit/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- In a heart‑disease cohort, retatrutide did **not** show a statistically significant reduction in MACE (CV death, MI, stroke) versus placebo.
- Weight loss and metabolic parameters improved, but the trial was not powered for definitive CV benefit; larger outcomes study needed.
- Advocacy group simultaneously petitioned FDA to ban DTC prescription‑drug ads, citing retatrutide’s promotional rollout.

**[Sean Kaufman’s ASPR confirmation vote postponed over vaccine‑safety statements](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/sean-kaufman-aspr-nomination-confirmation-vote-postponed-senate-committee/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- Senate committee delayed vote on Kaufman (nominee to lead ASPR) after scrutiny of past remarks questioning vaccine safety.
- Nomination now hangs on whether bipartisan support can be reassembled.

**[Timothy Westlake tapped to lead SAMHSA](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/trump-nominates-timothy-westlake-samhsa/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- Wisconsin emergency physician and addiction‑policy advisor nominated to head Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration.
- Brings state‑level opioid‑response experience; confirmation timeline unclear.

**[HHS convenes expert group on clinical AI standards](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/hhs-convenes-experts-on-clinical-ai-health-tech/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- Initiative aims to establish validation, transparency, and liability frameworks for AI/ML tools used in diagnosis and treatment selection.
- Includes industry, academia, and patient advocates; first deliverables expected within 12 months.

**[Bipartisan Senate bill proposes federal Office of Men’s Health](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/federal-office-mens-health-bipartisan-bill-filed-momentum-grows/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT
- Mirroring House legislation; mandates GAO study on U.S. men’s health status within one year.
- Would create permanent HHS office focused on sex‑specific research gaps and outreach.

**[Veterans who refused COVID vaccines still awaiting reinstatement](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/23/veterans-face-long-covid-vaccine-reinstatement-process/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT+
- Despite presidential directive, bureaucratic hurdles keep many former service members from rejoining.
- Process involves multiple waivers and medical reviews; completion rates remain low.

## Papers & Preprints

**[TextDTI: Multimodal context representation learning for drug–target interaction prediction](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42489038/)** — PubMed
- Integrates textual (literature, databases) and structural modalities in a unified transformer framework; benchmarks show improved DTI prediction across multiple target classes.

**[Dapagliflozin modulates diabetic kidney disease via indirect transcriptional regulation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42489012/)** — PubMed
- RNA‑seq in murine and human kidney models reveals SGLT2 inhibition acts through secondary metabolic signaling rather than direct reversal of disease‑associated transcriptomic signatures.

**[Structures and inhibition of Crimean‑Congo hemorrhagic fever virus polymerase](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486991/)** — PubMed
- Cryo‑EM structures of CCHFV L protein in complex with nucleoside analogs define binding pockets; informs broad‑spectrum antivirals against nairoviruses.

**[Docking of ultra‑large libraries yields neurotensin receptor agonists with analgesic activity](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486867/)** — PubMed
- Virtual screening of >1B compounds → synthetic validation → in vivo efficacy in rodent pain models; demonstrates feasibility of structure‑based discovery for challenging GPCR targets.

**[Deep learning identifies emodepside as a TNBC candidate](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486779/)** — PubMed
- Repurposing screen predicts anthelmintic emodepside inhibits TNBC growth via mitochondrial complex I; validated in PDX models.

**[Therapy‑induced senescence rewires melanoma secretome to enhance anti‑tumor immunity](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486609/)** — PubMed
- Senescent melanoma cells secrete CXCL9/10, recruiting CD8+ T cells; combining senescence‑inducing chemo with ACT improves tumor control in murine models.

**[HSP90 inhibition partially rescues splicing dysregulation in myotonic dystrophy models](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486259/)** — PubMed
- In DM1 cell models, HSP90i restores MBNL1 localization and corrects missplicing; suggests chaperone modulation as therapeutic avenue.

**[Second‑gen SERCA2a SUMOylation enhancer improves cardiac function in HF](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486183/)** — PubMed
- Metabolically stabilized compound enhances SERCA2a SUMO1 modification, improves Ca²⁺ handling and ejection fraction in rodent HF models.

**[Targeting antibody‑inaccessible immune resistance with small‑molecule immunotherapy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42486001/)** — PubMed
- Describes small molecules that disrupt intracellular immune‑evasion pathways (e.g., MHC‑I trafficking) unreachable by antibodies; early in vivo proof‑of‑concept.

**[Nature Reviews Disease Primers: Eosinophilic gastrointestinal diseases (Primer & PrimeView)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41572-026-00723-9)** — Nature Reviews Disease Primers
- Comprehensive overview of epidemiology, pathophysiology (type 2 inflammation, barrier dysfunction), diagnosis (histology thresholds), and management (diet, steroids, biologics).
- Highlights knowledge gaps: non‑invasive biomarkers, long‑term natural history, and pediatric‑to‑adult transition.

## Worth watching
FDA peptide panel verdict (compounding pathway vs. enforcement) and whether Lilly initiates a dedicated CVOT for retatrutide.
newsletter.subject: Knowledge Secretary — 2026-07-23

youtube.markdown:
- Biology & Health
    - [The Ultimate Peptide Stack? Gain Muscle, Lose Fat!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AvDrlcsdCBg) -- Physionic
        - The video examines combining GLP-1 receptor agonists (tirzepatide, semaglutide) with activin type II receptor inhibitors (apitegromab, bimagrumab) to achieve simultaneous fat loss and lean mass preservation or gain, citing Phase 2 trials showing bimagrumab plus semaglutide significantly reduced fat mass while increasing lean mass compared to semaglutide alone (Study 906) and apitegromab preserved lean mass during tirzepatide-induced weight loss (Study 905).
        - It details the mechanistic rationale for "replacing the 'A' with a 'B'" — shifting from selective myostatin (GDF8) inhibition to dual GDF8/activin A blockade via agents like bimagrumab (activin type IIA/IIB antibody) or apitegromab (pro-myostatin/activin A binding antibody), referencing preclinical data demonstrating this dual blockade prevents GLP-1–induced muscle loss while amplifying fat loss in obese mice and non-human primates (Study 909).
        - Direct clinical evidence is reviewed from a Phase 2 trial in type 2 diabetes with obesity where bimagrumab monotherapy reduced fat mass by ~20% and increased lean mass by ~3.6% over 48 weeks (Study 907), and a Phase 1 trial confirming GDF8 and activin A as primary negative regulators of muscle mass in postmenopausal women (Study 910).
        - Safety profiles are compared: bimagrumab showed dose-dependent adverse events including diarrhea, muscle spasms, and falls in the combination trial (Study 906), while apitegromab demonstrated a favorable safety profile in spinal muscular atrophy (Study 908) and the tirzepatide combination study (Study 905), with no significant increase in serious adverse events versus placebo.
        - The video highlights a potentially more effective strategy: extracellular blockade of latent myostatin/activin A activation (referencing Pirruccello-Straub et al., 2018) rather than receptor-level inhibition, which may offer superior tissue selectivity and avoid on-target side effects like bleeding or endothelial dysfunction associated with broad activin type II receptor blockade.
